tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Against All Odds: Cancer Survivor Conquers 100-Mile Mountain Race
stories.summary: Two-time cancer survivor Christina Klayko completed a grueling 100-mile ultramarathon just a year after hysterectomy and radiation, inspiring thousands with her resilience.

stories.headline: Small-Town Athletes Return as Champions, Fire Trucks Lead the Parade
stories.summary: Gabriella Baker, competing in her first Special Olympics, set a new USA Games record with an 800-meter time of 2:33.88, making history on her hometown's Fourth of July homecoming parade.

stories.headline: Two Strangers Rush Into Flames to Save Family
stories.summary: Mohsin Qayyum, 22, and Mohammed Yusuf Iqbal, 20, drove past a burning house in Leeds and ran across flaming roofs to rescue 77-year-old Sheila Robinson and her grandson.

stories.headline: Scientists Create the First Fully Synthetic Living Cell
stories.summary: University of Minnesota scientists created SpudCell, the first fully synthetic living cell with just 90 kilobase pairs of genetic data, defying the assumption that life requires at least 113.

stories.headline: Ronaldo Honors Fallen Teammate in Emotional World Cup Victory
stories.summary: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo scored Portugal's World Cup knockout win and tearfully wore late teammate Diogo Jota's No. 21 jersey, honoring him on the first anniversary of his death.

stories.headline: Restaurant Community Rallies to Aid Earthquake Survivors Across the Sea
stories.summary: Venezuelan restaurant owner Andrea Osorio mobilized volunteers through social media who showed up with tape and boxes to pack emergency supplies at Carupano Kitchen for earthquake survivors back home.

stories.headline: Police Officer's Kindness: Water Stations for Those in Need
stories.summary: A caring police officer bought cases of water during a brutal heatwave and set up stations across town to help unhoused people and those without access.

stories.headline: Britain's Native Crayfish Gets a Second Chance
stories.summary: Wildwood Trust released 70 endangered white-clawed crayfish into a Devon reserve, restoring Britain's only native freshwater crayfish to the wild after more than a year of careful habitat preparation.

stories.headline: Rare Fungi Hold Key to Restoring Island Rainforests
stories.summary: Scientists discovered symbiotic fungi critical to recovering native forests on remote Palmyra Atoll, offering real hope for reforestation efforts worldwide.

stories.headline: Solar Reactor Learns to Think Like a Plant—No Batteries Needed
stories.summary: Osaka researchers built a self-regulating solar electrolyzer that produces fuel from water and CO₂, adjusting to sunlight shifts through material physics alone.
